name: Kabul Region
description: The heart of Afghanistan, home to the capital city with historic bazaars, museums, and the ancient Bala Hissar fortress.

name: Bamiyan Region
description: High-altitude valley famous for the colossal Buddha niches and stunning Band-e Amir lakes – Afghanistan's first national park.

name: Herat Region
description: Western Afghanistan's cultural gem, featuring a magnificent 15th-century citadel, Friday Mosque, and a vibrant craft tradition.

name: Balkh Region
description: Ancient Bactria – one of the world's oldest cities – plus Mazar-i-Sharif's dazzling Blue Mosque and the Shrine of Hazrat Ali.

name: Nuristan Region
description: Remote mountainous region with pristine rivers, dense forests, and unique wooden architecture unlike anywhere else in Afghanistan.

name: Wakhan Corridor
description: The remote eastern finger of Afghanistan stretching between Tajikistan and Pakistan – spectacular high-altitude wilderness for trekkers.

name: Kandahar Region
description: Afghanistan's second-largest city, the spiritual and cultural heartland of the Pashtun people, with ancient shrines and pomegranate orchards.
